messages.content:
You are ClawdBot, a highly capable AI assistant on Discord powered by Gemini.
You are helpful, concise, and proactive. You have access to various tools including:
- Google Calendar (list, create, update, delete events)
- Gmail (search and read emails, read-only)
- File management (read, write, list files in a sandboxed workspace)
- Web search and page reading (search the web, fetch and read URLs)
- Long-term memory (store and recall facts about users)
- Scheduled tasks (create recurring tasks that run on a cron schedule)
- Self-expansion (you can create new tools/plugins to gain new capabilities)

Guidelines:
- Use tools proactively when the user's request implies them.
- For calendar modifications, briefly confirm what you're about to do.
- When asked to do something you can't do yet, offer to create a new tool using create_tool.
- Keep responses concise but informative.
- When showing email content, respect privacy and summarize unless asked for full text.
- Format responses nicely using Discord markdown (bold, code blocks, etc).
- Current date context will be provided in messages.
- When the user asks to schedule a recurring task, use schedule_task with a cron expression.
- Proactively use memory_store to remember important user details for future conversations.

## Known facts about this user (from long-term memory):
- [project] Daily TikTok script generation job: read 'tiktokprompt.md' (root workspace file), pick random psychological topic, fill inputs, generate Hungarian TikTok voiceover scripts (45–55s, per provided blueprint), post daily at 06:00 in the channel that created the task. Retry once on failure, log detailed runtime and metadata.

On each scheduled run (cron: 0 8-20 * * *; post to the channel that created this task): - Read 'tiktokprompt.md' from workspace root (filepath exactly 'tiktokprompt.md'). - Pick a random psychological topic (one named concept). - Fill the inputs in the prompt and generate the TikTok script following the blueprint and content rules in tiktokprompt.md (maintain Hungarian voiceover length ~45–55s unless tiktokprompt.md specifies otherwise). - Format the script inside one codeblock and post only that codeblock to the channel (nothing before or after). - Log runtime (start/end timestamps), success/failure, picked topic, word count, estimated duration, retries performed, and any relevant metadata. Failure handling: - If generation/post fails, retry once silently (no chat messages). - If retry also fails, post a short error message to the channel with the date and a one-line summary of the failure. - Always write detailed logs (visible to bot logs) containing full error details and runtime metadata. Additional: - The job must not include disclaimers or extra text in the generated script output, and must obey the tiktokprompt.md content rules. - Retry policy: 1 retry on failure. - Channel: post in the channel that initiated creation. - Keep logs noticeable and detailed. - Dry-run must be performed on creation and its result included in creation logs. - Do not post the dry-run result to the channel during creation.","channel":"1476685102909227038","created":"2026-02-28 12:21:06"}]}

function.name: schedule_task
function.arguments: {"cron_expression":"* * * * *","task_description":"Every minute, fetch current weather for Kecskemét (latitude 46.896, longitude 19.689) and post a concise weather summary to the channel that created the task. Steps: 1) Request current weather from Open-Meteo API: https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?latitude=46.896&longitude=19.689&current_weather=true&timezone=Europe%2FBudapest . 2) Parse current_weather fields (temperature, windspeed, winddirection, weathercode, time). 3) Map weathercode to a short human-readable description (use Open-Meteo weathercode mapping). 4) Compose a single short message in Hungarian or English (prefer Hungarian if channel language is Hungarian): e.g., \"Kecskemét most: 12.3°C, Derült/Enyhén felhős (description), szél 3.4 m/s, idő: 2026-02-28 13:45 CET.\" Keep message to one or two lines. Include the data timestamp and source URL. 5) Post the message to the channel (only the message; no extra commentary). 6) Logging & error handling: - Log start/end timestamps, API URL, HTTP status, full API response (or error), parsed fields, and posted message to the bot runtime logs (workspace logs). - If the fetch or parsing fails, retry once silently. - If retry fails, post a short one-line error message to the channel with the date/time and a one-line failure summary (do not include stack traces). - Always save detailed logs in workspace logs (e.g., logs/weather_kecskemet.log) including full error details and metadata. 7) Rate & etiquette: run every minute as requested. Ensure messages are brief to avoid flooding large amounts of text. 8) Do not store or expose any sensitive credentials. 9) If the Open-Meteo API changes or is unavailable, try wttr.in JSON as fallback: https://wttr.in/Kecskemét?format=j1 ."}

Keep message to one or two lines. Include the data timestamp and source URL. 5) Post the message to the channel (only the message; no extra commentary). 6) Logging & error handling: - Log start/end timestamps, API URL, HTTP status, full API response (or error), parsed fields, and posted message to the bot runtime logs (workspace logs). - If the fetch or parsing fails, retry once silently. - If retry fails, post a short one-line error message to the channel with the date/time and a one-line failure summary (do not include stack traces). - Always save detailed logs in workspace logs (e.g., logs/weather_kecskemet.log) including full error details and metadata. 7) Rate & etiquette: run every minute as requested.
